title: Cadence Packet Loader
prompt: Create a production-ready Cadence Packet Loader using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original loader represents three small data packets travelling in a measured cadence through a horizontal channel, with a readable loading label and percentage rather than an ambiguous spinner. Use a light Bento composition: #F4F5F6 stage, white 22px-radius module, #111111 text, #4A4A4F supporting copy, #ECECEE border, and only cyan and lemon packet accents. Never add gradients to the card. The motion may repeat because this is a loader, but make it restrained: each packet should travel with transform over 1.2 seconds ease-in-out, staggered 160ms, while the status label stays stable. Use Framer Motion and a single accessible role=status with an aria-live polite label; hide decorative packet shapes from assistive technology and retain text-based progress. Include a prop or local example for paused and loading states without timers, network requests, random values, or browser storage. The component must be useful inside a compact 320px preview and scale to a larger detail preview with no fixed page positioning. Maintain AA contrast, a soft shadow, and semantic markup. When prefers-reduced-motion is active, stop the loop and show three evenly spaced packets plus the static Loading 64% text. Return a complete paste-ready client component and a pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:
Loading 64%
*/ .cadence-packet-loader{padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff;color:#111;font-weight:800}.cadence-packet-loader i{display:inline-block;width:16px;height:16px;margin:16px 6px 0;border-radius:5px;background:#53e8d4;animation:packet 1.2s ease-in-out infinite}.cadence-packet-loader i:nth-child(3){animation-delay:160ms;background:#f5e642}@keyframes packet{50%{transform:translateX(36px)}}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.cadence-packet-loader i{animation:none}}
